title: [phase:plan] [family:orchestrator] startup bootstrap plan lane. Treat the current task only as a runtime startup canary. Do not inspect the repository. Stay live for bootstrap manifest only.
summary_text: # Plan Establish a minimal runtime startup canary manifest that proves the orchestration lane is live without inspecting repository contents or proposing implementation work. The approach is to define the bootstrap contract, success criteria, and observable checkpoints needed for a safe first signal. ## Scope - In: Startup canary manifest, lane readiness criteria, bootstrap-time signals, failure boundaries, operator-visible outputs - Out: Repository inspection, code changes, workflow edits, file-level implementation details, persistent config changes ## Action items [ ] Define the canary objective as “runtime startup only” and freeze all non-bootstrap concerns out of this lane [ ] Specify the bootstrap manifest fields: lane identity, phase, intent, execution mode, timestamp, and expected readiness state [ ] Define the minimum live signals required to consider startup healthy, including process alive, control-path reachable, and no hard-stop safety gate [ ] Add explicit success criteria for the canary: manifest emitted, readiness acknowledged, and no escalation triggered during bootstrap window [ ] Add explicit failure criteria for the canary: missing manifest, stalled startup, invalid lane identity, or bootstrap timeout [ ] Define the observation window and timeout policy for this startup check so the canary cannot hang indefinitely [ ] Record the operator response path for failure, limited to reporting bootstrap status and avoiding any repository or implementation action [ ] Verify the manifest remains ephemeral and runtime-only, with no dependency on repository reads, writes, or workflow mutation [ ] Close the lane after bootstrap evaluation with a single status outcome: `ready`, `degraded`, or `failed` ## Open questions - What bootstrap timeout should this canary use for the current runtime? - Should `degraded` be treated as pass-with-warning or as a failed startup signal? - Does the manifest need a correlation ID for downstream audit, or is lane identity plus timestamp sufficient?
